ccel.title: NPNF-212. Leo the Great, Gregory the Great

source.title: NPNF-212. Leo the Great, Gregory the Great

title: To Pelagius and Serenus, Bishops.

source.title: NPNF-212. Leo the Great, Gregory the Great

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Gregory to Pelagius of Turni and Serenus of Masilia (Marseilles) Bishops of Gaul.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Although with priests who have the charity that is well pleasing to God religious men need no commendation, yet, since an apt time for writing has offered itself, we have thought well to send a letter to your Fraternity, mentioning that we have sent into your parts, with the help of the Lord, for the benefit of souls, the servant of God Augustine, of whose earnestness we are assured, with other servants of God.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Him your Holiness must needs assist with priestly earnestness, and hasten to afford him your succour.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: We have also enjoined him, that so you may be the more ready to support him, to make you fully acquainted with the matter he has in hand, knowing that, when it is known to you, you will lend yourselves with entire devotion for God’s sake to succour him as the case requires.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Moreover, we commend in all ways to your charity our common son the presbyter Candidus, whom we have sent for the government of the patrimony of our Church.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Given on the tenth day of the Kalends of August, Indiction 14.
